focus: [D3-A6/D3] registry completeness + alias-collision static audit for sk-design hub

graphEvents.label: Can the sk-design registry prove every mode is reachable, every alias has one owner, and local intent coverage is measurable?
The live registry has five mode rows, five existing packet SKILL.md files, matching packetSkillName values, and zero normalized alias collisions
The raw hub keyword projection has 47 of 101 non-infrastructure keywords not covered by registry aliases, a 46.5% uncovered projection rate
The current skill-benchmark D5/router replay path parses INTENT_SIGNALS and RESOURCE_MAP, not hub mode-registry aliases or packet reachability
Hub-mode skills need a deterministic registry audit before packet router replay: mode parity, packet reachability, packet-name parity, alias uniqueness, and explicit corpus coverage

label: Registry passes narrow structural completeness: five modes, five existing packets, packet name parity, and zero alias collisions
Raw hub keyword projection has a 46.5% uncovered rate against registry aliases; several terms need typed wake/mode/bundle/non-routing classification before this becomes a real intent miss-rate
Existing skill-benchmark D5/router replay cannot detect registry mode loss, duplicate aliases, or hub keyword projection drift because it only parses INTENT_SIGNALS and RESOURCE_MAP
Context manifests should validate MODE BUNDLE LOADED entries against registry workflowMode keys and future route.workflowModes output, not free text alone
Hub-mode skills need a deterministic registry audit before packet router replay: mode parity, packet reachability, packet-name parity, alias uniqueness, and explicit corpus coverage

direction: Treat every hub keyword as a required registry mode alias
reason: The hub keyword projection contains tool, file, compatibility, and wake-only terms; forcing all of them into mode aliases would inflate routing noise instead of measuring uncovered user intent.
